representative_scenario.label: Representative Scenario
representative_scenario.description: A plausible creator-brand situation that feels realistic without pretending to be a named client case.

decision_math.label: Decision Math
decision_math.description: A simplified, creator-side calculation block that makes workload, payout, and break-even tradeoffs tangible.

clause_breakdown.label: Clause Breakdown
clause_breakdown.description: A sample risky clause, why it matters, and a safer rewrite or pushback version.

negotiation_script.label: Negotiation Script
negotiation_script.description: A copy-paste reply or pushback script a creator could realistically adapt.

disclaimer: These examples are representative teaching scenarios built to reflect common creator-brand workflows. They are not presented as audited client records or legal advice.
plausibilityRules: Do not claim a named creator, named client, or exact historical deal unless the article already includes that verified context.
Use realistic but clearly illustrative numbers, timelines, and workload assumptions.
Do not invent surveys, benchmarks, or claims about how often something happens unless the article already cites a real source.
If you provide a script, make it sound usable and grounded rather than theatrical or overly polished.
